project.name: Fullstack SIG (Sistem Informasi Geografis)
project.description: Web GIS application with 3 independent projects for geographic data visualization and management
project.author: Parsec - UNTAN Informatics Engineering

projects.name: WebGIS SPBU
projects.description: Gas stations distribution map with CRUD operations

projects.name: SIG Mapping Tanah & Jalan
projects.description: Land parcels and roads mapping with polygon/line drawing

projects.name: Poverty-Mapping GIS
projects.description: Social aid distribution mapping with community participation

1_prepare_files.description: Update database connection files to use environment variables
1_prepare_files.changes: Replace hardcoded DB credentials with getenv() calls

2_create_config_files.description: Create Docker and Railway configuration files

3_git_operations.description: Commit and push changes to GitHub

5_database_init.status: Ready to automate with MySQL CLI

critical: NEVER commit .env file with real credentials
Use .gitignore to prevent accidental secrets leak
recommended: Add input validation to all POST endpoints
Add rate limiting to prevent abuse

phase_1.name: Local Preparation
phase_1.description: Update files, create configs, commit to GitHub

phase_2.name: Railway Setup
phase_2.description: Create project, connect GitHub, add MySQL (needs UI interaction)

phase_3.name: Database Initialization
phase_3.description: Create databases, import schemas using MySQL CLI

phase_4.name: Verification
phase_4.description: Test endpoints with curl, verify deployment

bash_script.description: Automated bash script that handles Phase 1 + 3

python_helper.description: Python helper for Railway API integration (future)

phase_3: mysql -h $RAILWAY_HOST -u $RAILWAY_USER -p$RAILWAY_PASS -e 'CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS sig_spbu;'

database_connection_failed.cause: DB_HOST, DB_USER, or DB_PASS not set correctly
database_connection_failed.solution: Check Railway dashboard → PHP app → Variables tab

access_denied_for_user.solution: Copy correct MYSQLPASSWORD from Railway MySQL service variables

table_does_not_exist.solution: Run CREATE DATABASE and IMPORT commands again

cors_error_in_browser.solution: Already handled in koneksi.php - verify headers are present

context_for_claude_code.instructions: Use this manifest to understand the project structure and automate deployment. The automation should handle: 1) File updates with environment variable support, 2) Git operations, 3) Database initialization, 4) Testing. Some steps require manual interaction with Railway dashboard (Phase 2).
context_for_claude_code.key_insight: The project has 3 independent web apps sharing the same PHP/MySQL backend. Each needs its own database but uses the same codebase. Database connections use environment variables to support both local development (hardcoded defaults) and production (Railway-provided values).
context_for_claude_code.automation_level: ~80% automatable. Phase 2 (Railway UI) requires manual steps, but Phase 1, 3, and 4 are fully automated.
context_for_claude_code.estimated_total_time: 20 minutes including waiting for Railway build
